title: Ruling parties postpone reduction of seats to prioritize reform of the Imperial House Law
summary: The Liberal Democratic Party and the Japan Innovation Party agree not to approve the reduction of seats in the current legislative session and focus on reforming the Imperial House Law.

title: Nordic combined excluded from 2030 Winter Olympics
summary: The International Olympic Committee decides to remove Nordic combined from the 2030 Games program, a discipline in which Japan has won seven medals.

title: French justice convicts Marine Le Pen for embezzlement of European funds
summary: The appeals court upholds the conviction against the far-right leader, which could prevent her from running for the 2027 presidential election.

title: Fuji TV apologizes to actors Sato Jiro and Hashimoto Ai for incidents on set
summary: The Japanese network offers apologies following reports of inappropriate behavior during the recording of a drama, committing to improve safety.

title: Three people arrested for alleged illegal kidney trafficking in Cambodia
summary: Former directors of an NGO are arrested for brokering kidney transplants for money, in the first case of its kind in Japan.

title: Governor of Shizuoka authorizes start of construction for maglev train
summary: The Chūō Shinkansen project receives green light to begin construction in Shizuoka Prefecture, a step forward to connect Tokyo and Nagoya.

title: Yomiuri Giants defeat Hanshin Tigers with a double by Hayato Sakamoto
summary: The veteran player hits a decisive double in the eighth inning that gives the Giants victory in the top match of the Central League.

title: Argentina beats Egypt 3-2 and advances to World Cup quarterfinals
summary: With a goal from Lionel Messi that sets a new scoring record in World Cups, the Argentine team comes from behind and qualifies for the next round.

title: Naomi Osaka falls to Karolina Muchova in Wimbledon quarterfinals
summary: The Japanese tennis player loses in straight sets to the Czech and leaves the tournament without reaching the semifinals.

title: IOC authorizes participation of Russian athletes in international competitions
summary: The International Olympic Committee lifts restrictions on Russia but postpones the decision on the use of the flag and national anthem.

title: Diet begins debate to reform the Imperial House Law
summary: The House of Councillors committee begins hearings to amend the imperial succession law, with the opposition demanding transparency.

title: Trump praises Toyota's investment in the United States
summary: Former US President Donald Trump highlights Toyota's decision to expand its production in the US, attributing it to his economic policy.

title: Damaged Japanese vessels traverse the Strait of Hormuz amid tensions
summary: Thirteen Japan-related vessels, including a Mitsui O.S.K. Lines ship with two holes, manage to cross the strait amid regional tension.

title: Paraguayan senator condemned for racist comments against Kylian Mbappé
summary: The politician's discriminatory statements against the French footballer generate international condemnation and a reaction from the French government.

title: Earthquake in Venezuela causes death of volleyball national team captain
summary: A strong earthquake in Venezuela causes the death of the national volleyball team captain amid the rubble.

title: Japan's southernmost ski resort closes due to lack of snow
summary: The ski resort in Miyazaki Prefecture ceases operations due to global warming and a decline in visitors.

title: Kana Fukuma becomes challenger for the Hakurei shogi title
summary: The female five-title player earns the right to challenge the current champion, in a match that will define whether she obtains professional status.

title: China successfully conducts submarine-launched ballistic missile launch
summary: Beijing tests an SLBM as part of its nuclear deterrence strategy, while rejecting US pressure for arms control.

title: 25-year-old man arrested for hitting and killing a cyclist in Osaka
summary: Police arrest a suspect accused of fleeing after striking a woman riding a bicycle in Sennan, Osaka.

title: Nigel Farage resigns from British Parliament and will stand in a by-election
summary: The leader of the Reform UK Party resigns after a funding scandal but announces his candidacy for re-election in a new constituency.
